summary:
Subdural empyema can indeed result from complicated sinusitis, primarily through hematogenous dissemination along valveless diploic veins; studies show it occurs in up to 80% of cases linked to otolaryngology infection. This makes it one of the most common non-traumatic origins compared to surgical injury which contributes less commonly at around 20%.

Only summarize what was reasoned about — DO NOT add new information beyond this passage.

Subdural empyema can occur as a complication of sinusitis due to bacterial spread via valveless diploic veins from infected paranasal sinuses. Up to 80% of cases associated with otorhinologic infections involve sinusitis, making it a frequent cause—more so than traumatic or post-surgical origin.
